data.title: Krystal Biotech projects EU and Japan launches for VYJUVEK in 2025, targeting $1B global peak sales
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Krystal Biotech (NASDAQ:KRYS) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Krish Krishnan highlighted 2024 as a strong year, driven by the continued success of the VYJUVEK launch in the U.S., which is tracking as a top-tier rare disease launch. He emphasized the company’s ability to deliver genes to the lung with a nebulizer in programs addressing cystic fibrosis, alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency (A1ATD), and non-small cell lung cancer, validating the lung as a second therapeutic target.
  • Key goals for 2025 include successful VYJUVEK launches in the EU and Japan, translating early signs of efficacy into strong results across the CF and A1AT programs, and preparing for the launch of B-VEC to treat ocular complications in DEB patients.
  • CFO Kate Romano reported $91.1 million in net VYJUVEK revenue for Q4 2024, contributing to $290.5 million for the year. Gross margins remained consistent at 95%. Net income for the quarter was $45.5 million, or $1.58 per basic share.

Outlook

  • Management reiterated its confidence in achieving a global peak sales target of over $1 billion for VYJUVEK. The company expects a positive CHMP opinion in February, setting the stage for a mid-year launch in Germany and subsequent launches in France and Japan.
  • The company aims to initiate the Phase 3 IOLITE study for KB803 in the first half of 2025, with top-line data expected before year-end. This program targets ocular complications in DEB patients.
  • Operating expenses for 2025 are projected between $150 million and $175 million, excluding stock-based compensation, driven by ex-U.S. launch costs and expanded pipeline activities.

Financial Results

  • Krystal Biotech reported $91.1 million in Q4 2024 VYJUVEK revenue vs. analysts’ revenue estimate of $91.35 million. Adjusted EPS was $1.58 per basic share vs. analysts’ EPS estimate of $1.85.
  • The company ended the quarter with $344.9 million in cash and $749.6 million in total cash, short-term, and long-term investments, representing a quarterly increase of $55 million.
  • Research and development expenses were $13.5 million, up from $11.4 million in Q4 2023, attributed to increased trial, manufacturing, and regulatory costs.

Q&A

  • Alec Stranahan, BOA: Asked about year-end stocking for VYJUVEK and its impact on sequential revenue growth. Krish Krishnan clarified that there were no significant changes in inventory levels and stated that revenue volatility is managed through careful accrual processes.
  • Ritu Baral, TD Cowen: Inquired about pricing and patient access in Europe. Krish Krishnan explained that Germany launches with the U.S. price for six months, followed by pricing negotiations, while in France, the AP2 program mandates early pricing adjustments. Pricing in Europe is expected to range between 50% and 70% of the U.S. price.
  • Andrea Newkirk, Goldman Sachs: Asked about infrastructure needs for the European launch. Krish Krishnan noted that each European country will require a team of fewer than 10 employees, with Germany’s team already in place and plans for France and Japan to follow.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns about patient compliance trends and their potential impact on VYJUVEK’s revenue. Questions reflected a cautious tone regarding ex-U.S. launch readiness and competition in the DEB market.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing strong demand and robust operational execution. Krish Krishnan frequently expressed optimism about achieving peak sales targets, while also addressing potential headwinds like holiday-related treatment disruptions.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts displayed heightened interest in the European and Japanese launches, reflecting increasing anticipation of revenue growth from these regions.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 2024 saw a sequential increase in VYJUVEK revenue from $83.8 million in Q3 2024, supported by continued reimbursement approvals and a growing prescriber base. Gross margin improved slightly to 95% from 92% in the prior quarter.
  • Management’s tone remained consistent, with a continuing focus on ex-U.S. launches and pipeline advancements. The projected European and Japanese launches were emphasized more prominently this quarter.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted toward the impact of upcoming competition in the DEB market and the dynamics of pricing negotiations in Europe.

Risks and Concerns

  • The potential impact of competition in the DEB market was highlighted, with management acknowledging the need for strong patient and physician engagement to maintain market share.
  • Delays in regulatory approvals or launches in Europe and Japan could impact revenue projections.
  • Analysts expressed concern about compliance trends as some patients transition to maintenance regimens, potentially reducing treatment frequency.
  • Manufacturing scale-up for VYJUVEK remains a critical area, though management stated that recent FDA-approved process changes mitigate risks.

Final Takeaway

Krystal Biotech continues to demonstrate strong performance with VYJUVEK, achieving sustained revenue growth and high gross margins. The company’s focus on ex-U.S. launches in Europe and Japan, coupled with advancements in its pipeline, positions it for significant revenue expansion in 2025. While challenges like competition and compliance trends remain, management’s confidence in achieving $1 billion in global peak sales underscores its long-term growth potential.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: EnWave Corporation announces equipment purchase agreement, license amendment with Sprouted Proteins SAC
data.description:
• EnWave Corporation (TSXV:ENW:CA) signed an equipment purchase agreement and a license amendment with Sprouted Proteins SAC of Peru.
  • Sprouted Proteins signed a royalty-bearing license agreement with EnWave on February 26, 2024.
  • The amendment grants Sprouted Protein with the exclusive right to produce certain starch-based snack products and instant soup mixes in Peru and sets annual minimum exclusivity royalty payment amounts payable to EnWave.
  • Sprouted Proteins has purchased a 10kW REV machine and is leasing a second 10kW REV machine to ramp up commercial production.
  • Pursuant to the amendment, Sprouted Proteins must purchase a large-scale REV machine prior to September 30, 2025, to keep their exclusive license rights.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: SunCar Technology Group expands partnership with China Pacific Insurance, adding two provinces
data.description:
• SunCar Technology Group (NASDAQ:SDA) has secured new contracts with China Pacific Insurance Company's (CPIC) Zhejiang and Jiangsu provincial branches.
  • These agreements establish SunCar as the primary car wash and detailing service provider for CPIC's customers in these regions.
  • The expansion brings SunCar's total CPIC provincial partnerships to ten major regions.
  • The company has set a target to double its CPIC coverage to 20 provinces by year-end 2025.
  • SDA shares rise 3.4% on Wednesday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Devon Energy raises dividend by 9.1% to $0.24
data.description:
• Devon Energy (NYSE:DVN) declares $0.24/share quarterly dividend, 9.1% increase from prior dividend of $0.22.
  • Forward yield 2.5%
  • Payable May 1; for shareholders of record March 14; ex-div March 14.
  • See DVN Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Orion Energy Systems commences on-site LED lighting installation for federal government agency
data.description:
• Orion Energy Systems (NASDAQ:OESX) has commenced on-site LED lighting installation for a federal government agency.
  • Project purchase orders to date total ~$3M, with future potential for an expanded project scope.
  • The project is expected to be completed in the first half of Orion’s FY’26.
  • Orion is delivering a turnkey solution for a Southeastern U.S. facility, including LED lighting retrofit, electrical upgrades, and project management services.
  • OESX shares fall 2.3% on Wednesday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Wix targets $2B revenue in 2025, eyes AI-driven growth and new product launches
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Wix.com (NASDAQ:WIX) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Avishai Abrahami highlighted Wix's achievement of the Rule of 40 milestone one year ahead of schedule and announced a new goal of achieving Rule of 45 in 2025. He cited strong execution and innovation as drivers of the company’s performance, with bookings growing 18% year-over-year in Q4, and Partners revenue climbing 30% year-over-year.
  • Abrahami detailed the impact of AI developments, including the AI Website Builder, which has facilitated the creation of over 1 million sites. Additionally, the launch of monetized AI tools like AI Site Chat and the AI Marketing Agent is expected to enhance user engagement and drive incremental revenue.
  • COO Nir Zohar emphasized the focus on onboarding high-intent users, noting a 13% year-over-year increase in average bookings per subscription to over $294. He also pointed out the expansion of product offerings in commerce and AI tools as key contributors to growth.
  • CFO Lior Shemesh reported Q4 bookings of $465 million and revenue of $460 million, with free cash flow at $132 million or 29% of revenue. He outlined plans to pay down the $575 million convertible note due in August 2025 and highlighted the company’s aim to generate $590 million to $610 million in free cash flow for 2025.

Outlook

  • Wix projects full-year 2025 revenue to range between $1.97 billion and $2.00 billion, representing 12% to 14% growth year-over-year. On a constant currency basis, this growth is expected to increase to 14% to 16%.
  • Q1 2025 revenue is anticipated at $469 million to $473 million, with 12% to 13% growth year-over-year as reported.
  • The company plans to achieve non-GAAP gross margins of 70% for 2025, driven by efficiencies from AI cost initiatives in the second half of the year.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 revenue reached $460 million, up 14% year-over-year, driven by both Self Creators and Partners businesses. Partners revenue surged 29% to $168 million, while transaction revenue totaled $57 million, reflecting a 12% increase in GPV.
  • Full-year 2024 revenue was $1.76 billion, a 13% year-over-year increase, with creative subscriptions ARR growing to $1.343 billion by year-end.
  • Free cash flow for 2024 was $488 million, nearly doubling from 2023 and representing 28% of revenue.

Q&A

  • Ygal Arounian, Citigroup: Asked about 2025 guidance acceleration and premium subscriber growth. CFO Lior Shemesh attributed momentum to products like Studio and AI tools, noting that premium subscribers are expected to show positive trends in 2025.
  • Elizabeth Porter, Morgan Stanley: Inquired about the new Self Creator solution and its expanded design capabilities. CEO Avishai Abrahami explained the tool would allow users to create content for platforms beyond websites, leveraging AI for ease of use and increased efficiency.
  • Deepak Mathivanan, Cantor Fitzgerald: Sought insights on the roadmap for AI agents and Studio adoption. Abrahami highlighted early success with monetized AI tools and Studio’s ability to streamline workflows for agencies, leading to better efficiency and superior website designs.
  • Josh Beck, Raymond James: Questioned the future of Studio’s editor and GPV growth. COO Nir Zohar confirmed ongoing product innovation and noted that Partners now contribute over 50% of GPV, with expectations for continued growth.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a slightly positive tone, focusing on the momentum in AI tools, Studio’s adoption, and strategic growth initiatives. Questions centered on new product launches and the trajectory of Self Creators and Partners.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, particularly in addressing AI-driven conversion improvements and upcoming product launches. Phrases like “we are very excited” and “we are confident” underscored bullish sentiment.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone showed heightened enthusiasm, especially regarding the achievement of the Rule of 40 milestone and the 2025 outlook.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2025 revenue growth remained consistent with prior optimism but included explicit FX impact considerations.
  • Strategic focus has shifted more prominently to new AI tools and Self Creator product innovations, compared to the prior quarter's emphasis on Studio and foundational AI developments.
  • Analysts continued to probe on AI monetization and GPV growth, but recurring questions about Studio’s traction highlighted sustained interest in its momentum.
  • Management’s confidence has increased, bolstered by stronger Q4 performance metrics and the successful early rollout of AI-powered tools.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged elevated FX volatility as a potential headwind for 2025 results, with approximately $34 million in anticipated revenue impact.
  • Slightly lower GPV growth in Q4 due to holiday timing and FX challenges could persist if similar conditions recur.
  • Analysts raised concerns about premium subscriber growth and adoption rates for new products, with management acknowledging these as areas of focus for 2025.

Final Takeaway

Wix closed 2024 with impressive growth across key metrics, achieving its Rule of 40 target ahead of schedule and setting its sights on Rule of 45 for 2025. With strong momentum from AI-driven innovations, Studio adoption, and new product launches, the company is poised for sustained growth. Despite FX headwinds, management expressed confidence in delivering robust revenue and profitability improvements, supported by a streamlined cost structure and expanding customer base.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Columbia Sportswear, Intuitive Machines expand scientific partnership ahead of lunar mission
data.description:
• Columbia Sportswear (NASDAQ:COLM) expanded its partnership with space exploration company Intuitive Machines ahead of its second lunar launch later this month.
  • Columbia’s Omni-Heat Infinity and Omni-Shade Sun Deflector technologies will help protect the lander from the extreme cold and hot temperatures in space and on the Moon.
  • The goal of the mission to the Moon’s South Pole is to drill into the surface to search for lunar ice, a feat that has never been attempted in this inhospitable environment.
  • Omni-Heat Infinity heat-reflective insulation will help protect part of the lander from freezing cold temperatures, while Omni-Shade Sun Deflector technology will help shield part of Athena from the sun’s extreme heat in space and on the lunar surface.
  • Liftoff of the IM-2 lunar mission is targeted for a multi-day launch window that opens no earlier than February 26 from NASA’s Kennedy Space Center.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Hudbay Minerals plunges on softer gold production guidance
data.description:
Hudbay Minerals (NYSE:HBM) -16.7% in Wednesday's trading to its lowest in five months after reporting lower Q4 net income and revenues while also saying it expects lower gold production in FY 2025 than in 2024.

Q4 net income attributable to owners fell to $21.2M, or $0.05/share, from $30.7M, or $0.10/share, in the year-earlier quarter, while revenues declined 2.9% to $584.9M from $602.2M a year ago.

For FY 2025, Hudbay (NYSE:HBM) forecast gold production of 247.5K-308K oz, well below last year's output of 332,240 oz, consolidated copper production is expected at a range of 117K-149M metric tons, compared with 137,943 tons in 2024, and silver output is seen at 3.52M-4.39M oz, vs. 3.98M oz produced last year.

Hudbay (HBM) also said its Copper World project in Arizona is now fully permitted, and is expected to increase consolidated copper production by more than 50% from current levels once in production.

According to Bloomberg, Scotiabank analyst Orest Wowkodaw said Hudbay's (HBM) updated 2025 guidance is slightly below expectations due to a weaker outlook in Peru, due to slowly advancing growth plans in the country.

The analyst said guidance figures for copper and gold were 7% and 4% below the bank's estimates based on respective midpoints of 143K tons and 290K oz, driven by a weaker than expected outlook in Peru, particularly lower overall contribution from the Pampacancha mine.

data.title: Apple unveils 'more affordable' iPhone 16e, starting at $599
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) unveiled its highly awaited iPhone 16e on Tuesday, aimed at consumers looking for a “more affordable” iPhone experience.

Apple shares fell fractionally in late morning trading.

The new iPhone, which is available starting on Feb. 28, offers Apple Intelligence, Apple's A18 chip, better battery life — up to six hours longer than iPhone 11 and up to 12 hours longer than all generations of iPhone SE — and a 48MP 2-in-1 camera system, the company said in a release. It also offers the all-new Apple C1 chip, the first cellular modem designed by Apple.

The modems of previous iPhones were designed by Qualcomm (QCOM).

The latest Apple smartphone also sports Face ID and has wireless charging and USB-C connection.

“iPhone 16e packs in the features our users love about the iPhone 16 lineup, including breakthrough battery life, fast performance powered by the latest-generation A18 chip, an innovative 2-in-1 camera system, and Apple Intelligence,” said Kaiann Drance, Apple’s vice president of Worldwide iPhone Product Marketing. “We’re so excited for iPhone 16e to complete the lineup as a powerful, more affordable option to bring the iPhone experience to even more people.”

The iPhone 16e starts at $599 and is available in white and black colors in 128GB, 256GB, and 512GB storage.

CEO Tim Cook teased the launch last week, writing “Get ready to meet the newest member of the family” on X.

Apple first introduced the iPhone SE in 2016, but a lack of features have led to slow sales of the device. The company most recently refreshed the iPhone SE in 2022.

The launch of the iPhone 16e comes at a time when Apple is looking to reinvigorate revenue growth from the iconic device, which was first announced in 2007. Revenue attributed to the iPhone fell 1% year-over-year in the most recent quarter to $69.14B, below the $71.04B estimate.

data.title: Sekur Private Data plans to uplist on Nasdaq
data.description:
• Sekur Private Data (OTCQB:SWISF) announced that the company is advancing toward planned uplisting to Nasdaq from the OTC Markets platform.
  • SWISF selected an investment banking firm and are in the final stages of negotiations to formalize the engagement.
  • The roadmap targets a successful uplisting by the end of Q2, 2025.
  • In 2025, SWISF will launch the Sekur Corporate Suite.
  • SWISF shares rise 6% on Wednesday.
  • Source: Press release
